LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS NOW SUPPORTED BY A PROPER SUPPLEMENTAL AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT FROM ATTORNEY PAUL MANASIAN. THE MOTION WAS "MADE PURSUANT TO THE MANDATORY PROVISION" OF CCP 473(B) ON THE GROUNDS OF "ATTORNEY MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR NEGLECT." THUS, ATTORNEY MANASIAN SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL $10,000 IN "REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS." (SEE ID.) VERIFIED ANSWER IS TO BE FILED BY DECEMBER 21, 2018. LLC MOVES FOR MANDATORY RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B) APPARENTLY FOR HER FAILURE TO BRING TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION AT THE MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 HEARING THE FACT THAT SHE HAS SUBMITTED A SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY FOR FILING AT 9:24 P.M. ON FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 6, 2019. COUNSEL EXPLAINS THAT SHE BELIEVED THAT THE MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER (BASED ON LACK OF REPRESENTATION FOR A CORPORATE DEFENDANT) CALENDARED FOR SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 HAD BEEN TAKEN OFF CALENDAR ON AUGUST 27, 2019 BY VIRTUE OF SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF TAKING OFF CALENDAR OF A MOTION FOR AND ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, TERMINATING SANCTIONS, STRIKING DEFENDANTS ANSWER (BASED ON THE PROVISIONS OF THE DISCOVERY ACT AND FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY) PURPORTEDLY CALENDARED FOR SEPTEMBER 17, 2019. COUNSEL DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW SHE MISTOOK ONE MOTION FOR ANOTHER CONSIDERING DIFFERENT GROUNDS FOR RELIEF AND DIFFERENT HEARING DATES FOR THE MOTIONS. IT IS ALSO NOT EXPLAINED BY COUNSEL WHY SHE DID NOT INQUIRE FURTHER REGARDING SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 HEARING HAVING RECEIVED AN E-MAIL FROM OPPOSING COUNSEL ON SEPTEMBER 6, 2019 STATING: "THE COURT ISSUED A TENTATIVE RULING ON FRIDAY GRANTING THE BANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE XELAN'S ANSWER THAT IS SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY AT 9:30 A.M, WHICH WAS NOT DISPUTED. IF YOU HAVE AN ISSUE WITH THAT, YOU CAN APPEAR AND MAKE YOUR POSITION KNOWN TO THE COURT." HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL GRANT THIS MOTION FOR RELIEF BECAUSE, HAD COUNSEL APPEARED AT THE HEARING TO ALERT THE COURT OF THE FILING OF THE SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, THE MOTION TO STRIKE WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR COSTS AND FEES WILL BE CONTINUED TO JANUARY 16, 2020 AT 9:30AM. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Notice Of Motion For Termination Of Receivership Release Of Remaining Receivership Estate Funds And Authorization To Claim Deficiency In Related Proceeding
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing RE: OTHER KEVIN SINGER's Notice Of Motion For Termination Of Receivership Release Of Remaining Receivership Estate Funds And Authorization To Claim Deficiency In Related Proceeding.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Receiver Kevin Singer's motion to terminate the receivership, release the remaining receivership estate funds, and authorization to claim a deficiency in a related proceeding is granted.

The receivership is terminated. No party opposes and there is nothing left for the Receiver to do in this case.

Plaintiff ("the Bank"), the Receiver, and counsel (Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP ("ECJ")) are owed $28,598.82, $51,002.24, and $96,076.10, respectively. (Singer Decl., pars. 7, 11, Exs. 6 and 7.) The Receiver currently holds $42,807.75 and there are insufficient funds to service the outstanding debt. The court orders a pro rata distribution as follows: $6,977.66 to the Bank, $12,414.25 to the Receiver, and $23,415.84 to ECJ.

The Receiver is given leave to assert a claim in City and County of San Francisco v. Kihagi, et al., SFSC Case No. CGC-15-546152 (the "CCSF Case"). Defendant's assets are under receivership in the CCSF Case and that court can decide whether it is equitable and proper to allow further distribution to the Bank, the Receiver, and ECJ.

The court rejects defendant's request to re-litigate matters that have been firmly settled in this case, e.g., the propriety of the appointment of the Receiver on April 6, 2018.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk : V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Orders Granting Plaintiffs April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Application Effectively Denying The Borrowers Statutory Right Of Redemption As Void And Contrary To Law
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Karen Uchiyama; Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC.
Robert B. Kaplan; Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP; 415-398-8080; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of plaintiff Umpqua Bank.
Blake C. Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of receiver Kevin Singer.

Court reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com, 760-805-3974.

Counsel for defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDERS GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE APPLICATION EFFECTIVELY DENYING THE BORROWER'S STATUTORY RIGHT OF REDEMPTION AS VOID AND CONTRARY TO LAW is DENIED. Nothing of the face of the "judgment roll" indicates that April 22, 2019 order is void. April 22, 2019 order concerned an unauthorized encumbrance in violation of a Court order; determination regarding stay requirements on appeal and authorization to obtain counsel. The order did not make determinations regarding right of redemption which is purportedly the basis for the moving party's argument that the order is void.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com, 760-805-3974. (D501)

Motion To Vacate April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Order On Grounds That The Motion Was Procedurally Defective Resulting In A Void Order
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Karen Uchiyama; Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC.
Robert B. Kaplan; Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP; 415-398-8080; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of plaintiff Umpqua Bank.
Blake C. Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of receiver Kevin Singer.

Court reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com, 760-805-3974.

Counsel for defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE ORDER ON GROUNDS THAT THE MOTION WAS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE RESULTING IN A VOID ORDER is DENIED. There is nothing on the face of the "judgment roll" indicating that the judgment is void. On April 19, 2019 the ex-parte application was submitted for filing. The ex-parte calendar starts at 9:00 a.m. in the morning and parties are required to provide courtesy copies of their filings in support and in opposition (if any) to the Court. The Court hears the applications and reviews the courtesy copies. Apparently on April 22, 2019 a clerk of the Court reviewed and rejected the entirety of the filing due to formatting issues because a cover page was missing on the exhibits submitted for filing separately from the application itself. The formatting error was rectified in the re-submitted application and it appears on file with a May 7, 2019 filing date. There is no dispute that the application was submitted for filing and copies were provided to the Court and the parties in advance of April 22, 2019 hearing. All other arguments brought in this motion sound like a motion for reconsideration and do not support a finding of voidness (e.g. "the key declarations, were riddled with evidentiary insufficiencies, as the declarations were conclusory, based upon hearsay, and layered with suppositions.").

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com, 760-805-3974. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Allowance And Payment Of Its Attorneys Fees And Costs From The Receivership Estate As A Prevailing Party And As The Holder Of A Claim Against The Receivership Estate
Off Calendar Nov-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Blake C. Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of Receiver Kevin Singer.
Robert B. Kaplan; Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP; 415-398-8080; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of plaintiff Umpqua Bank.
Ariel Gershon; Greenstein & McDonald; 415-773-1240; appearing via Zoom on behalf of Third Parties/Objectors Dale Duncan and Marta Munoz Mendoza.

No other appearances.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ALLOWANCE AND PAYMENT OF ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS FROM THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE AS A PREVAILING PARTY AND AS THE HOLDER OF A CLAIM AGAINST THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE is GRANTED. Moving party established its entitlement to recovery of attorney's fees in this action and supported its request for payment of attorney's fees incurred in other cases out of the receivership estate. The amount of fees (including hourly rate and hours spent re: loadstar determination) is supported by evidence and legal authority. Late-filed, oversized opposition without tables of contents and authorities is stricken. Opposition was due on October 18, 2021 and was submitted for filing at 11:03 p.m. on October 20, 2021. Late-filed and oversized opposition did not support its arguments relevant to this motion with any authority on point (e.g. authority standing for the proposition that request for payment of fees out of receivership estate incurred in other cases must be made in a separate motion; authority that the Court should not enter an order authorizing recovery of fees because an appeal from such order may be taken; etc). Opposition did not specify a single line-item in the billing statements reflecting overbilling for a particular task performed (unreasonable rate or unreasonable hours for a task).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion For Mandatory Relief From Order Striking Answer And Entering Default And Entry Of Default Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Per Ccp 473b
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Joshua Haevernick, 415.398.8080 for Plaintiff Umpqua Bank.
Karen Uchiyama, 415.563.9300 for Defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC.

Argued. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC. Motion For Mandatory Relief From Order Striking Answer And Entering Default And Entry Of Default Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Per Ccp 473b is GRANTED. Counsel for Xelan Prop 1. LLC moves for mandatory relief under CCP 473(b) apparently for her failure to bring to the Court's attention at the Monday, September 9, 2019 hearing the fact that she has submitted a Substitution of Attorney for filing at 9:24 p.m. on Friday, September 6, 2019. Counsel explains that she believed that the Motion to Strike Answer (based on lack of representation for a corporate defendant) calendared for September 9, 2019 had been taken off calendar on August 27, 2019 by virtue of service of the Notice of Taking off Calendar of a Motion for and Order Deeming Admissions Admitted, Terminating Sanctions, Striking Defendants Answer (based on the provisions of the Discovery Act and failure to comply with discovery) purportedly calendared for September 17, 2019. Counsel does not explain how she mistook one motion for another considering different grounds for relief and different hearing dates for the motions. It is also not explained by counsel why she did not inquire further regarding September 9, 2019 hearing having received an e-mail from opposing counsel on September 6, 2019 stating: "The Court issued a tentative ruling on Friday granting the Bank's motion to strike XELAN's Answer that is set for hearing on Monday at 9:30 a.m, which was not disputed. If you have an issue with that, you can appear and make your position known to the Court." However, the Court will grant this motion for relief because, had counsel appeared at the hearing to alert the Court of the filing of the Substitution of Attorney, the motion to strike would not have been granted. Plaintiff's request for costs and fees will be continued to January 16, 2020 at 9:30am. Opposition due by January 8, 2020, repy due by January 10, 2020. Proposed order submitted. Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
